name: Cyril — 72, comfortable income, the clawback case
note: Winter Fuel Payment is paid to him automatically like everyone else — and recovered in full by HMRC because his own taxable income is over £35,000. The verdict says both halves, and that opting out exists. His income also puts Pension Credit out of reach, which the card confirms.
checked: Recovery rule against gov.uk/winter-fuel-payment (individual £35,000 threshold, partner income not counted; opt-out exists), 2026-07-12.

winter-fuel-payment.because: £200 for your household this winter, paid automatically — but HMRC recovers the share of anyone whose own taxable income is over £35,000, through PAYE or Self Assessment. You can opt out of receiving it instead.
